mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) reflects the real economy's output capacity. A rising trend indicates robust economic activity, driving corporate earnings expectations upward and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This signal is always active as it captures the secular growth trajectory.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the 60-day forward return conditional on INDPRO rising is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above a threshold of 5.0%, it signals a weakening labor market and rising recession risk. Historically, this leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as corporate earnings downgrades and risk aversion intensify.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% in May 2022 is below threshold but rising
failure_condition: Over any 5-year period where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% at least 10 times, the average 20-day forward return after trigger is not negative or the hit rate is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a tightening monetary policy regime. This interaction compresses equity valuations via higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If in periods where both CPIAUCSL is above 300 and FEDFUNDS above 3%, the 20-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, or if the interaction term in a regression (CPIAUCSL*FEDFUNDS) on forward returns has a t-statistic less than 2 in absolute value over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During an expansionary regime characterized by low unemployment, robust retail sales (RSAFS) signal strong consumer demand and economic momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is regime-conditional: it is only active when UNRATE is below 4.5%.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return following RSAFS increases in the low-unemployment regime is not significantly higher than in the high-unemployment regime (difference of means t-test p > 0.05), or if the rank IC is below 0.05 in the regime over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A week-over-week increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending. This always-on trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to the improving economic narrative.
failure_condition: If over any 5-year period the average 5-day forward return following a positive PAYEMS change is not positive, or the rank IC is below 0.03 on a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% signals a housing market contraction, which historically leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month due to spillover effects on construction, employment, and financial sector health.
failure_condition: If, in periods where HOUST drops >10% MoM (at least 10 occurrences in the sample), the median 20-day forward return is not negative or the proportion of negative returns is less than 60%.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) stagnates or declines while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains high, the economy faces a stagflationary environment. The combination of weak output and high prices erodes corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, creating a negative outlook for equities over the next month.
failure_condition: If in periods where INDPRO growth is below 0% and CPIAUCSL inflation is above 5% YoY, the 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or the interaction term coefficient in a regression is not significant at the 5% level over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle (defined by consecutive FEDFUNDS rate hikes), rising short-term interest rates increase discount rates and tighten financial conditions, compressing equity valuations. This regime-conditional signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as the full effect of tighter policy feeds through.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.64% in May 2022 indicating ongoing tightening
failure_condition: If during episodes of sustained FEDFUNDS increases (at least three consecutive hikes) the mean 60-day forward return is not negative, or the hit rate for negative returns is below 55% over all tightening cycles in the dataset.
